title: BGP Neighbor Down / Route Flap Response
trigger_conditions: BGP peer DOWN notification, hold-timer expired, prefix withdrawal spike
step_2: Check BGP neighbor state: vtysh -c 'show bgp summary'
step_3: Review hold-timer config: vtysh -c 'show bgp neighbors <ip>'
step_4: Check firewall/ACL on port 179: iptables -L | grep 179
step_5: Enable route dampening if flapping: vtysh -c 'conf t' -c 'router bgp 65000' -c 'bgp dampening'
step_7: Soft-clear BGP session: vtysh -c 'clear bgp neighbor <ip> soft'
step_8: Escalate to ISP if physical layer suspected. Reference ISP NOC: +91-XXX-XXXXXXX

title: Interface Congestion / Bandwidth Saturation Response
trigger_conditions: Bandwidth util >80%, queue depth >75%, QoS policy breach
step_1: Identify top talkers: show interfaces | grep rate, check NetFlow dashboard
step_2: Check QoS policy: vtysh -c 'show policy-map interface'
step_3: Apply emergency rate-limit on bulk class: vtysh -c 'conf t' -c 'policy-map BRANCH-OUT' -c 'class BULK' -c 'police rate 50 mbps'
step_4: Check for scheduled backup jobs: contact server team via Teams/Slack
step_5: Reroute non-critical traffic to alternate path if available
step_6: Monitor utilization every 2 minutes until below 70%
step_7: Schedule capacity upgrade ticket if sustained for >30 minutes
step_8: Post-incident: adjust backup job scheduling to off-peak hours

title: MPLS Tunnel / IPSec Degradation Response
trigger_conditions: Tunnel health <80%, DPD failures >3, rekey anomaly, packet loss >2% on tunnel
step_1: Check tunnel IKE state: show crypto isakmp sa, show crypto ipsec sa
step_2: Verify LDP sessions: vtysh -c 'show mpls ldp neighbor'
step_3: Test path quality: ping <remote_endpoint> repeat 200 size 1400
step_4: Check underlay latency: traceroute mpls ipv4 <dest>
step_5: Pre-initiate IKE rekey if SA near expiry: crypto isakmp key rekey
step_6: Switch to backup path: vtysh -c 'ip route <dest> <backup_next_hop> 5'
step_7: Contact ISP if underlay quality issue confirmed
step_8: Check BFD configuration: vtysh -c 'show bfd peer'

title: OSPF Adjacency Loss Response
trigger_conditions: OSPF neighbor FULL→DOWN, dead interval expired, Hello packets not received
step_1: Check OSPF neighbor state: vtysh -c 'show ip ospf neighbor'
step_2: Verify Hello interval match: vtysh -c 'show ip ospf interface'
step_3: Check MTU on both sides (most common cause): ip link show <iface>
step_4: Verify area IDs match: vtysh -c 'show ip ospf'
step_5: Check authentication keys if configured: vtysh -c 'show ip ospf interface <iface>'
step_6: Clear OSPF process if stuck: vtysh -c 'clear ip ospf process'
step_7: Set MTU to 9000 on both sides if mismatch: ip link set <iface> mtu 9000

title: Packet Loss / Interface Error Investigation
trigger_conditions: Packet loss >1%, CRC errors, input errors rising, BFD echo failures
step_1: Identify affected interface: show interface | grep error
step_2: Run extended ping to isolate hop: ping <dest> repeat 500 size 1400
step_3: Check SFP optical power levels: show inventory, show controller
step_4: Verify speed/duplex: show interface <iface> | grep duplex
step_5: Check for microbursts in traffic pattern: show queue stats
step_6: Inspect input/output queue drops: show interface <iface> counters
step_7: Replace SFP if optical power below -20 dBm
step_8: Escalate to physical layer team if CRC errors persist

title: QoS Policy Drift / Misconfiguration Response
trigger_conditions: DSCP remarking mismatch, VoIP quality degraded, QoS policy not matching baseline
step_1: Compare running config vs approved baseline: diff <running> <baseline>
step_2: Identify changed policy maps: show policy-map | diff vs last-known-good
step_3: Revert to approved template: configure replace flash:qos-baseline.conf
step_4: Verify DSCP markings: show policy-map interface <iface>
step_5: Test VoIP quality: run IP SLA VOIP probe to branch endpoint
step_6: Document change in ITSM: who changed, when, reason
step_7: Enable change management alert for policy-map changes
